title: Fix CategoryPage infinite scroll product disappearing during pagination

requirements.text: Remove the complex buffering pattern from CategoryPage infinite scroll: eliminate `pendingProductsRef`, `apiFetchDoneRef`, `timerDoneRef`, and all associated conditional logic that defers product rendering.

quotes: Remove Complex Buffering: Eliminate the pending products buffer pattern
Remove pendingProductsRef, apiFetchDoneRef, timerDoneRef, and related logic

acceptanceCriteria: `pendingProductsRef`, `apiFetchDoneRef`, and `timerDoneRef` refs are removed from CategoryPage
No conditional logic gates product rendering behind timer or API completion flags
Component compiles and renders without errors after removal

requirements.text: Refactor the product accumulation logic in CategoryPage so that products are appended immediately when the API responds, using a functional state update with barcode-based deduplication. Remove the page 0 vs page 1+ branching — always use append logic regardless of page number.

quotes: Always append products immediately regardless of page number
Use functional update to safely append to existing array

acceptanceCriteria: When a new page of products is fetched, they are immediately appended to `accumulatedProducts` without waiting for any timer
Page 0 and page 1+ use the same append code path
Barcode-based deduplication prevents duplicate products from appearing in the grid
Existing products never disappear or reset during pagination

requirements.text: Decouple the loading spinner (`isLoadingMore`) from product visibility in CategoryPage. The spinner should start when a page load is triggered, run for a minimum display duration on its own independent timer, and hide only after that timer expires — regardless of when the API response arrives or when products are rendered.

quotes: Only hide spinner when timer expires
Products visible during spinner display period
Loading spinner visible for minimum duration even if products already showing

acceptanceCriteria: `isLoadingMore` is set to `true` when a load-more is triggered and only set to `false` after the minimum duration timer expires
Products are visible in the grid while the spinner is still showing
Spinner does not block or delay product rendering
Spinner remains visible for the full minimum duration even if the API responds quickly

requirements.text: Retain `processedPagesRef` in CategoryPage to prevent the same page from being processed twice, and ensure stable product keys in ProductGrid (keyed by barcode).

quotes: Maintain processedPagesRef to prevent processing same page twice
Ensure stable product keys in ProductGrid

acceptanceCriteria: `processedPagesRef` is still present and prevents duplicate page processing on re-renders
ProductGrid renders each product with a stable key based on its barcode
Scrolling through multiple pages does not produce duplicate product cards

requirements.text: Preserve all Spanish-language interface messages in CategoryPage (loading text, error messages, empty state messages) exactly as they currently appear. Do not modify `frontend/src/index.css` — only add new CSS classes if strictly necessary.

quotes: Do not modify index.css styles/classes, only add new ones if necessary

acceptanceCriteria: All user-visible text in CategoryPage remains in Spanish
No existing CSS classes in `frontend/src/index.css` are modified or removed
New CSS classes may only be added if required by the refactored component structure

constraints: This is a frontend-only change — do not modify backend/main.mo or any backend files
Do not modify frontend/src/index.css — only add new CSS classes if strictly necessary
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
nonGoals: Changes to any admin pages or admin product management
Changes to the homepage infinite scroll (HomepageCategoriesSection)
Backend data model or API changes
Any new product features or UI redesign
